in_scope: Implement internal/debate signal parsing for a trailing fenced signal block containing JSON fields position string, objections []string, and done bool.
Implement an internal/debate PromptBuilder assignable to orchestrate.PromptBuilder, with Delta and Full render modes.
Implement an internal/debate Verdict assignable to orchestrate.Verdict, configurable for until values all_done and quorum.
Add unit tests under internal/debate using internal/engine/transport/mock scripted responses through orchestrate.Run.
Preserve the dependency direction: internal/debate may depend on internal/engine, while internal/engine remains debate-agnostic.
out_of_scope: Real acp, exec, or api transport implementations and external model calls.
CLI behavior, command flags, stdout/stderr UX, and cmd/debate changes.
.heurema/debate discovery, config loading, persona parsing, and persona file handling.
Nudge-retry orchestration for unparsed signals; this slice only parses and treats unparsed signals as not done.
acceptance: Given turn text ending with a fenced signal block labeled signal containing JSON {"position":"ship","objections":[],"done":true}, the parser returns a typed signal with those values and parsed_ok true.
Given missing, non-trailing, malformed, non-JSON, or wrong-typed signal blocks, the parser returns parsed_ok false and does not panic.
Given a parsed signal with done true and one or more objections, the parser normalizes the result so done is false while preserving the parsed objections.
The prompt builder output contains the task/context brief, moderator rules of engagement, a transcript board, and the signal-format instruction for every rendered turn.
In Delta mode, the prompt builder renders the board from Transcript.DeltaFor for the speaking participant; in Full mode, it renders the full transcript from Transcript.All.
The verdict returns Clean true for all_done only when every current-round speaker has a parsed signal with done true.
The verdict returns Clean true for quorum only when a strict majority of current-round speakers have parsed signals with done true.
The verdict treats any speaker with an unparsed or invalid signal as not done.
The verdict sets Progress true only when the current open-objection set differs from the previous assessed round's open-objection set.

Mock-backed orchestrate.Run tests cover settled convergence after the configured settle streak, quorum convergence, stalemate on a frozen objection set, max-round termination, and unparsed-signal handling.
All new tests run without real backend processes, network access, API keys, persona files, or .heurema/debate configuration.
internal/engine packages do not import internal/debate or product-layer packages.
assumptions: Quorum means a strict majority: done_count > total_speakers/2.
Open-objection identity is exact string equality after trimming surrounding whitespace; duplicate objections are coalesced for set comparison.
Only turns from the current RoundContext.Round are assessed for the current verdict; prior assessments are used only to compare the previous open-objection set.
Prompt wording may be deterministic implementation text as long as the required sections and Delta/Full transcript sources are observable in tests.
